title: Google Just Dropped a Masterclass on Agentic Engineering (It's SO Good)
summary: Cole Medin discusses Google's 50-page playbook on agentic engineering, arguing that the AI model accounts for only 10% of coding outcomes while the surrounding harness of context, tools, guardrails, and verification comprises the other 90%. The video covers the spectrum from vibe coding to agentic engineering, the importance of context engineering, and the evolution of developer roles from conductor to orchestrator.
writeup: Cole Medin walks through Google's 50-page playbook on agentic engineering, centering on the claim that the model drives only 10% of coding outcomes while the surrounding harness of context, tools, guardrails, and verification accounts for the other 90%. The video maps the spectrum from vibe coding to agentic engineering and stresses context engineering as the core discipline. It frames the developer's role as shifting from conductor to orchestrator, with verification and context management as the levers that matter most.
